markschemeHtml:
EITHER

(though statistically significant) the new technique only seems to increase the time before cracks appear by 1 hour out of 250, so it is not a significant increase (i.e. the effect size is small)          R1

OR

the minimum time (not mean time) before cracks appear should be considered given the context / An appropriate confidence interval should be considered, and not simply the mean.          R1

 

Note: If a not significant p-value was seen in part (h), do not award R1 for an answer of “the result is not significant” in part (i).

[1 mark]
